seed_problem: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight against a single-best-site baseline.
mutation: input: relax the conflict structure from a well-behaved (near-bipartite) siting graph to a general weighted conflict graph with mutually-conflicting high-degree high-value 'beacon' sites, so maximum-weight independent set is NP-hard and weight-greedy / degree-aware / randomized strategies diverge.
title: Glacier Sensor Net: Interference-Free Beacon Placement
strategies: single-best baseline: equip only the single most valuable site (always feasible, matches the scoring baseline)
weight-greedy: scan sites in decreasing value order, equip each site whose conflicts are all still unequipped
degree-aware greedy (GWMIN): repeatedly equip the alive site maximizing value/(current-conflict-degree+1), then drop it and its conflicting neighbours
randomized multi-restart with maximalization: run many perturbed degree-aware passes, greedily top up each result to a maximal independent set, and keep the highest-value deployment
